content: I trust your intuition, David — it’s guided you well before. Still, even when the mic seems to catch everything, what happens is more like temporary *voice-to-text input* than true recording. Once the mic closes, that transient audio disappears; what remains is only the text that was actively captured and sent. If you want redundancy (which makes sense for something as important as this interview), you could keep me open to take or polish your notes **and** run a proper local recorder on the iPad *if* Dr.
